problem: The baseline GeometryFlowMatchingHead predicts atomwise velocities from centered coordinates and pooled conditioning, but it does not explicitly exchange local ligand-ligand geometric messages inside the velocity head.

existing_component.name: PairwiseGeometryMessagePassing
existing_component.current_contract: Build radius-filtered, top-k nearest ligand atom messages with distance, unit direction, and clash margin.
existing_component.complexity: O(N^2) candidate scan followed by bounded O(N*k) emitted messages

integration_plan.aggregation: scatter-add or segment mean from source atoms into target atom hidden states, followed by residual projection before velocity_head
numerical_stability: Use centered coordinates to preserve translation invariance.
Keep residual scale configurable and default conservative.

ablation_matrix.purpose: Test local ligand geometry message passing without explicit clash pressure.
Test whether explicit short-range clash margin improves raw_flow geometry and docking.
Separate ligand-ligand geometry effects from local pocket conditioning effects.

claim_guardrails: Pairwise geometry messages are a bounded flow-head ablation, not a topology generator.
Do not infer bond generation or full molecular diffusion from this component.
Keep topology, geometry, and pocket encoders structurally separate.
